title: Simplex Method for Linear Programming
description: Solve linear programming problems using simplex algorithm
mermaid: graph TD A1[Define Problem: Objective function, constraints, non-negativity] style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff B1[Convert to Standard Form: Introduce slack, surplus, artificial variables] style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 C1[Initialize Tableau: Basic feasible solution] style C1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 D1[Calculate Reduced Costs: cj - zj for each variable] style D1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff E1{Optimality Check: Are all reduced costs <= 0 (for maximization) or >= 0 (for minimization)?} style E1 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff F1[Select Entering Variable: Most negative (maximization) or most positive (minimization) reduced cost] style F1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff G1[Calculate Ratios: Divide solution column by entering variable column (only for positive denominators)] style G1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff H1[Select Leaving Variable: Row with smallest non-negative ratio] style H1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff I1[Perform Row Operations: Make entering variable column a unit vector (pivot)] style I1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff J1[Update Tableau: New basic feasible solution] style J1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 K1{Unboundedness Check: Are all ratio denominators <= 0?} style K1 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff L1[Optimal Solution Found: Read variable values and objective function value from tableau] style L1 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff M1[Problem is Unbounded: Solution is infinite] style M1 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff A1 --> B1 B1 --> C1 C1 --> D1 D1 --> E1 E1 -- Yes --> L1 E1 -- No --> F1 F1 --> G1 G1 --> H1 H1 --> I1 I1 --> J1 J1 --> D1 G1 --> K1 K1 -- Yes --> M1 K1 -- No --> H1
entities: Define Problem: Objective function, constraints, non-negativity
Convert to Standard Form: Introduce slack, surplus, artificial variables
